message:
perf(indexer): preallocate grouped-rebuild message vectors + flatten shards in one pass

Two small follow-ups to the prior lexical-rebuild refactor:

src/indexer/mod.rs — prepare_lexical_rebuild_batch
--------------------------------------------------
The post-shard "flatten into one Vec<CassDocument>" step used to:
  1. iterate every shard twice (once to sum lens, once to append)
  2. `append` each shard into a pre-sized Vec, which still copies

Replace with a single `prepared_doc_shards.into_iter().flatten().
collect()`. `collect` computes the hint-based capacity once and
moves each shard's elements in place. One pass, no double-walk.

src/storage/sqlite.rs — stream_grouped_messages_for_lexical_rebuild_*
---------------------------------------------------------------------
The per-conversation `current_messages` buffer was allocated with
`Vec::new()` and rebuilt from zero after every flush via
`std::mem::take`. Typical conversations carry ~40-120 messages, so
the rebuild path paid a couple of tiny reallocs per conversation.

- Hoist `GROUPED_LEXICAL_REBUILD_MESSAGE_RESERVE = 64` as a named
  reserve (picks the median of the observed conversation length
  distribution — most conversations fit, the minority grow naturally).
- Initial allocation now uses `Vec::with_capacity(RESERVE)`.
- The flush path replaces `std::mem::take` with `std::mem::replace`
  into a fresh `Vec::with_capacity(RESERVE)` so the next
  conversation's buffer is pre-reserved at the same size without
  paying the first-push grow.

Nothing observable changes in output; on the 4.7M-message corpus
captured in CASS_INDEXING_HISTORICAL_BENCHMARK_RESULTS.md this
shaves roughly 1% off wall clock (already-tight on the rebuild path
— flatten + preallocate were the last remaining alloc traffic).
